---
title: "Onion Skin View Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# OnionSkinViewWidget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Onion Skin View Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

## Completed Work Summary

The following features are complete and documented in [index.qmd](index.qmd):

- **Widget skeleton**: EditorState pattern, registration, state/view/properties factories
- **State management**: `OnionSkinViewState` with `HorizontalAxisState`, `VerticalAxisState` composition; `OnionSkinViewStateData` serializable struct with `rfl::json`
- **Three data type support**: Add/remove multiple `PointData`, `LineData`, and `MaskData` keys via properties panel
- **Temporal window**: Configurable `window_behind` / `window_ahead` (frames before/after current time)
- **Alpha curve**: Linear, Exponential, Gaussian curves with configurable min/max alpha
- **Rendering pipeline**: `SceneRenderer` for points (glyphs), lines (polylines), and mask contours (polylines), all with per-element temporal alpha
- **Spatial mapping**: `SpatialMapper::mapPointsInWindow()`, `mapLinesInWindow()`, `mapMaskContoursInWindow()` for windowed data retrieval
- **Back-to-front rendering**: Depth-sorted by temporal distance for correct alpha blending
- **Current-frame highlight**: Distinct colors for current-frame elements (orange-red vs blue/green/orange)
- **Auto-fit bounds**: Bounding box computation from all windowed point, line, and mask contour data
- **Axis system**: `HorizontalAxisWidget` (X) and `VerticalAxisWidget` (Y) with bidirectional sync, including the "silent update" pattern
- **Interaction**: Separated X/Y zoom, pan via shared `PlotInteractionHelpers`
- **Entity selection**: Click-to-select point at current frame (15px tolerance), double-click for navigation
- **Properties panel**: Data key tables + combo boxes for all 3 types, temporal window controls, alpha curve controls, rendering controls (point size, line width, highlight current), axis range controls in collapsible sections
- **DataManager observer**: Combo box population (point, line, mask) and cleanup in destructor
- **State serialization**: Full round-trip JSON via `rfl::json`
- **Registration wiring**: `timeChanged` → `_onTimeChanged()`, `timePositionSelected` → `EditorRegistry::setCurrentTime()`

**Not yet implemented**: Per-key visual customization (glyph type/color/size per point key, line color/thickness per line key, contour color/thickness per mask key), SVG export, and the cross-widget shared options infrastructure described below.

---

## Phase 1: Per-Key Point Appearance Options

**Status**: **Partially complete** — global glyph customization (shape, size, color, alpha) is
implemented using the shared `CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData` struct and the `GlyphStyleControls`
widget from `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/`. Per-key options are not yet implemented.

**What was implemented**:

- `OnionSkinViewStateData::point_glyph_style` (`CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData`) replaces the
  former `float point_size` field — serializable with reflectcpp.
- `GlyphStyleState` (QObject wrapper) composed into `OnionSkinViewState`, with `styleChanged`
  wired to `stateChanged()` + `markDirty()` for automatic scene rebuilds.
- `GlyphStyleControls` widget embedded in `OnionSkinViewPropertiesWidget`, replacing the old
  point-size spinbox.
- Rendering in `OnionSkinViewOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()` uses
  `CorePlotting::toRenderableGlyphType()` and `CorePlotting::hexColorToVec4()` from
  `GlyphStyleConversion.hpp`. The glyph style's color/alpha serves as the base, with temporal
  alpha blending applied on top.

**Remaining work** (per-key options):

Extend `OnionSkinViewStateData`:

```cpp
struct OnionSkinPointKeyOptions {
    std::string data_key;
    PointGlyphOptions glyph; // glyph_type, glyph_size, hex_color
};

struct OnionSkinViewStateData {
    // Replace: std::vector<std::string> point_data_keys;
    // With:
    std::vector<OnionSkinPointKeyOptions> point_keys;
    // ...
};
```

Add per-key signals to `OnionSkinViewState`:

- `pointKeyOptionsChanged(QString const & key)` — emitted when glyph options for a specific key change

### 1.3 Properties Panel Controls

Replace the current single-column point data table with a multi-column table:

| Column | Control | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Data Key | Label | Read-only key name |
| Glyph | Combo | Circle, Square, Diamond, Cross, Triangle |
| Size | Spinbox | Point size in pixels |
| Color | Color button | Opens `QColorDialog` |

On table row selection change, update inline editors or a detail area below the table.

### 1.4 Rendering Update

In `OnionSkinViewOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()`, replace the hardcoded blue color and
uniform size with per-key values from state:

```cpp
for (auto const & key_opts : _state->getPointKeyOptions()) {
    auto point_data = _data_manager->getData<PointData>(key_opts.data_key);
    if (!point_data) continue;

    auto pts = SpatialMapper::mapPointsInWindow(*point_data, center, behind, ahead);
    auto base_color = hexToGlmVec4(key_opts.glyph.hex_color);

    for (auto const & pt : pts) {
        float alpha = computeTemporalAlpha(pt.offset, half_width, alpha_curve, min_alpha, max_alpha);
        // Use key's glyph_size and glyph_type, modulated by temporal alpha
        glm::vec4 color = glm::vec4(base_color.r, base_color.g, base_color.b, alpha);
        // ... add to scene with key_opts.glyph.glyph_type, key_opts.glyph.glyph_size
    }
}
```

---

## Phase 2: Per-Key Line Appearance Options

**Status**: Not started. Lines currently render with a single global thickness and hardcoded
green base color. Thickness and color are not configurable per key.

**Goal**: Allow the user to configure line appearance (thickness, color) independently for
each added `LineData` key, aligning with per-series options in
[LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) (thickness + color per series) and planned for
[TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 2).

### 2.1 Shared Line Options Struct

A shared line options struct should be extracted (see [Phase 6](#phase-6-shared-visual-options-infrastructure)):

```cpp
struct LineRenderOptions {
    std::string hex_color = "#33B366"; ///< Line color as hex string
    float line_width = 2.0f;          ///< Line thickness in pixels
};
```

The same struct is needed by:

| Widget | Current Line Support | Planned per-key |
|--------|---------------------|-----------------|
| [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) | Per-series thickness + color (`LinePlotOptions`) | Done |
| [TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) | Global only | Planned (Phase 2) |
| **OnionSkinViewWidget** | Global only | This phase |

### 2.2 Per-Key State Extension

Extend `OnionSkinViewStateData`:

```cpp
struct OnionSkinLineKeyOptions {
    std::string data_key;
    LineRenderOptions line; // hex_color, line_width
};

struct OnionSkinViewStateData {
    // Replace: std::vector<std::string> line_data_keys;
    // With:
    std::vector<OnionSkinLineKeyOptions> line_keys;
    // ...
};
```

### 2.3 Properties Panel Controls

Replace the current single-column line data table with a multi-column table:

| Column | Control | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Data Key | Label | Read-only key name |
| Width | Spinbox | Line thickness in pixels |
| Color | Color button | Opens `QColorDialog` |

### 2.4 Rendering Update

In `rebuildScene()`, apply per-key line color and width. Each line segment's color includes
the base key color modulated by temporal alpha:

```cpp
for (auto const & key_opts : _state->getLineKeyOptions()) {
    auto line_data = _data_manager->getData<LineData>(key_opts.data_key);
    if (!line_data) continue;

    auto lns = SpatialMapper::mapLinesInWindow(*line_data, center, behind, ahead);
    auto base_color = hexToGlmVec4(key_opts.line.hex_color);

    for (auto const & ln : lns) {
        float alpha = computeTemporalAlpha(ln.offset, half_width, alpha_curve, min_alpha, max_alpha);
        glm::vec4 color = glm::vec4(base_color.r, base_color.g, base_color.b, alpha);
        // ... add polyline to scene with key_opts.line.line_width and color
    }
}
```

---

## Phase 3: Per-Key Mask Contour Appearance Options

**Status**: Not started. Mask contours currently share the global line width and render with
a hardcoded orange base color.

**Goal**: Allow the user to configure mask contour appearance (thickness, color) independently
for each added `MaskData` key. Because masks are rendered as contour polylines, they use the
same `LineRenderOptions` struct as line data (see [Phase 2](#phase-2-per-key-line-appearance-options)).

### 3.1 Per-Key State Extension

```cpp
struct OnionSkinMaskKeyOptions {
    std::string data_key;
    LineRenderOptions contour; // hex_color, line_width — same struct as line keys
};

struct OnionSkinViewStateData {
    // Replace: std::vector<std::string> mask_data_keys;
    // With:
    std::vector<OnionSkinMaskKeyOptions> mask_keys;
    // ...
};
```

### 3.2 Properties Panel Controls

Replace the current single-column mask data table with a multi-column table:

| Column | Control | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Data Key | Label | Read-only key name |
| Width | Spinbox | Contour line thickness in pixels |
| Color | Color button | Opens `QColorDialog` |

### 3.3 Rendering Update

Identical pattern to Phase 2 — per-key contour color and width, modulated by temporal alpha.

### 3.4 Migration of Global Rendering Controls

After Phases 1–3, the global `point_size` and `line_width` properties become redundant (replaced
by per-key options). These global controls should be removed from the properties panel and state
struct, or repurposed as a "default" value used when adding new keys.

---

## Phase 4: SVG Export

**Status**: Not started. No export infrastructure exists in the current widget.

**Goal**: Allow the user to export the current onion skin view as an SVG file, preserving
vector quality for publication.

### 4.1 Export Approach

SVG export requires converting the OpenGL scene to vector graphics. Two approaches:

**Option A — Scene-to-SVG serialization** (recommended): Walk the `RenderableScene` primitives
(glyphs, polylines) and emit SVG `<circle>`, `<polygon>`, and `<polyline>` elements with the
same colors and alpha values computed during `rebuildScene()`. This avoids OpenGL framebuffer
capture and produces clean, resolution-independent output.

**Option B — QPainter-based rendering**: Use `QSvgGenerator` + `QPainter` to re-render the scene
data using Qt's 2D drawing API. This provides more mature vector rendering but requires
duplicating the rendering logic outside OpenGL.

### 4.2 SVG Export Infrastructure

Because SVG export will be needed by multiple widgets (see cross-widget export below), the
export logic should be implemented as a shared utility rather than per-widget code:

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `SvgExporter` | `CorePlotting/Export/` | Scene-to-SVG conversion |
| `ExportButton` | `Plots/Common/` | Shared "Export SVG" button + file dialog |

SVG export is also a planned feature for:

| Widget | Export Complexity | Notes |
|--------|------------------|-------|
| [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) | Medium | Trial-aligned lines + axes |
| [TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) | Medium | Full spatial overlay |
| **OnionSkinViewWidget** | Medium | Windowed spatial + alpha fading |

### 4.3 Export Flow

```
User clicks "Export SVG" button in properties panel
        ↓
File dialog → choose output path
        ↓
SvgExporter reads current RenderableScene (or re-gathers data from state)
        ↓
For each glyph: emit SVG element with position, size, fill color + opacity
For each polyline: emit SVG <polyline> with stroke color + opacity + width
        ↓
Add axis labels, tick marks, bounds metadata
        ↓
Write SVG file
```

### 4.4 Properties Panel Controls

Add an "Export" section to the properties panel:

- **Export SVG button** — opens file dialog and triggers export
- **Include axes checkbox** — whether to render axis labels and ticks in the SVG
- **Background color** — whether to include the dark background or use transparent

---

## Phase 5: DataManager Observer Improvements

**Status**: Not started. The current observer repopulates combo boxes but does not handle
stale visualization keys.

**Goal**: Implement stale key purging when data keys are deleted from the DataManager, following
the pattern recommended in the [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md).

### 5.1 Stale Key Purging

When the DataManager observer fires, after repopulating combo boxes, check whether any
currently added data keys have been deleted:

```cpp
void OnionSkinViewPropertiesWidget::_onDataManagerChanged() {
    _populatePointComboBox();
    _populateLineComboBox();
    _populateMaskComboBox();

    // Purge stale point keys
    auto all_keys = _data_manager->getAllKeys();
    std::set<std::string> valid(all_keys.begin(), all_keys.end());

    for (auto const & key : _state->getPointDataKeys()) {
        if (valid.find(key.toStdString()) == valid.end()) {
            _state->removePointDataKey(key);
        }
    }
    // Repeat for line and mask keys...
}
```

### 5.2 Template-Based Common Tests

Add `OnionSkinViewPropertiesWidget` to the template test suite in
`tests/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/Common/PlotWidgetCommonTests.hpp` by providing a traits class:

```cpp
struct OnionSkinViewPropertiesTraits {
    using WidgetType = OnionSkinViewPropertiesWidget;
    using StateType = OnionSkinViewState;
    // ...
};
```

This verifies combo box population, observer refresh, destruction cleanup, and stale key handling.

---

## Phase 6: Shared Visual Options Infrastructure

**Status**: **Partially complete** — the core shared glyph infrastructure has been implemented.

The following shared components now exist and are used by OnionSkinViewWidget,
TemporalProjectionViewWidget, and EventPlotWidget:

| Component | Location | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| `CorePlotting::GlyphType` enum | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleData.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData` struct | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleData.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::toRenderableGlyphType()` | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleConversion.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::hexColorToVec4()` | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleConversion.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `GlyphStyleState` | `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/Core/GlyphStyleState.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `GlyphStyleControls` | `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/GlyphStyleControls.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `LineRenderOptions` struct | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/` | Not started |
| `LineOptionsWidget` | `Plots/Common/` | Not started |
| `ColorButton` | `Plots/Common/` | Not started |

### 6.2 Shared UI Widgets

Create reusable property editor widgets:

**`GlyphOptionsWidget`** — A single row or compact section with: glyph type combo, size spinbox,
color button. Emits `optionsChanged(PointGlyphOptions)`. Used inline in per-key data tables.

**`LineOptionsWidget`** — A single row or compact section with: width spinbox, color button.
Emits `optionsChanged(LineRenderOptions)`. Used inline in per-key data tables.

These widgets would replace the ad-hoc inline editors in each properties panel, ensuring
consistent look and behavior across:

- OnionSkinViewWidget (this widget)
- [TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 1, 2)
- [ScatterPlotWidget](../ScatterPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 4)
- [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 3)

### 6.3 Migration Path

1. Extract `PointGlyphType` and `PointGlyphOptions` to `CorePlotting/DataTypes/`
2. Extract `LineRenderOptions` to `CorePlotting/DataTypes/`
3. Create `GlyphOptionsWidget` and `LineOptionsWidget` in `Plots/Common/`
4. Refactor existing per-key tables in [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) to use shared widgets
5. Implement Phases 1–3 of this roadmap using the shared infrastructure
6. Adopt in TemporalProjection / ScatterPlot / EventPlot as those roadmap phases are reached

---

## Priority Order

| Priority | Phase | Dependency |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| 1 | Phase 1 — Per-Key Point Options | None (can use local structs first) |
| 2 | Phase 2 — Per-Key Line Options | None |
| 3 | Phase 3 — Per-Key Mask Options | Phase 2 (shares `LineRenderOptions`) |
| 4 | Phase 4 — SVG Export | Phases 1–3 (export should capture per-key styles) |
| 5 | Phase 5 — Observer Improvements | None |
| 6 | Phase 6 — Shared Options Infrastructure | Phases 1–3 inform the shared design |

Phases 1–3 can be implemented with widget-local structs first, then refactored to use the
shared infrastructure from Phase 6 once the pattern stabilizes across widgets.
